productCatalogFailure.description: Fail product catalog service on a specific product

adManualGc.description: Triggers full manual garbage collections in the ad service

adHighCpu.description: Triggers high cpu load in the ad service

kafkaQueueProblems.description: Overloads Kafka queue while simultaneously introducing a consumer side delay leading to a lag spike

cartFailure.description: Fail cart service n% of the time

paymentFailure.description: Fail payment service charge requests n%

loadGeneratorFloodHomepage.description: Flood the frontend with a large amount of requests.

imageSlowLoad.description: slow loading images in the frontend

failedReadinessProbe.description: readiness probe failure for cart service

emailMemoryLeak.description: Memory leak in the email service.

intlShippingSlowdown.description: Delays international shipping responses to simulate overseas shipping delay

loadGeneratorSeasonality.description: Drives load generator traffic along a periodic diurnal-style wave with the given cycle length in minutes, producing forecastable demand curves for predictive AI scenarios (pre-warm scaling, seasonal SLO/consumption baselines)

productCatalogSlowQuery.description: Simulates a missing-index / query-plan regression in the product catalog Postgres database: each catalog read runs an inefficient full-scan + sort workload, driving up query latency and CPU. Severity scales the number of rows scanned. Surfaces as a top statement in pg_stat_statements and slow database calls in Dynatrace.

productCatalogConnectionPoolExhaustion.description: Simulates Postgres connection-pool exhaustion at scale: the product catalog holds its pooled connections open inside idle-in-transaction backends, starving real requests until they time out. Reproduces 'remaining connection slots' pressure, idle-in-transaction backends, and connection-acquisition timeouts.

productCatalogQueryStorm.description: Simulates the N+1 / ORM lazy-loading anti-pattern in the product catalog: listing products issues an additional per-product query instead of a single set-based read, flooding Postgres with a storm of small queries and inflating query throughput and connection churn.

productCatalogLogFlood.description: Log Management demo: the product catalog emits N additional log records per request, simulating a chatty/debug-logging regression that inflates log ingest volume and cost and drowns real signal. The variant is the number of extra records per request.

productCatalogMalformedLogs.description: Log Management demo: the product catalog emits structurally broken log records (truncated JSON, embedded newlines, unbalanced delimiters), reproducing a broken structured-logging deploy that breaks downstream log parsing/OpenPipeline processing.

productCatalogMemoryLeak.description: Infrastructure demo: the product catalog retains memory on every request, producing a gradual heap-growth trend (distinct from the abrupt emailMemoryLeak) that drives memory-saturation forecasting, anomaly detection, and eventual container OOMKill. The variant is the megabytes retained per request.

checkoutConversionDrop.description: Business Observability demo: the checkout silently abandons a fraction of orders before payment with a benign non-error outcome (recorded as demo.order.outcome=abandoned on the order metric and an order.abandoned log event), producing a pure conversion/funnel dip with no technical error to trace. Extract as a bizevent in OpenPipeline.

checkoutRevenueLeak.description: Business Observability demo: the checkout silently undercharges each order by the given fraction, so order volume holds steady while revenue per order (demo.order.amount on spans, logs and the revenue counter) drops - a margin/revenue-leakage anomaly visible only in business data. Extract as a bizevent in OpenPipeline.

aiAgentRunawayLoop.description: AI Observability demo: the shop assistant performs N extra backend tool calls (list_products) after answering, reproducing a runaway agent stuck in a tool-calling loop. Inflates gen_ai tool-call spans and drives load on downstream services. The variant is the number of extra tool iterations.

aiAgentSlowResponse.description: AI Observability demo: the shop assistant injects the given number of seconds of latency into its workflow, simulating slow model/provider responses and inflating gen_ai request duration. The variant is the delay in seconds.

frontendJsErrorStorm.description: Frontend/RUM demo: the storefront raises a stream of handled JavaScript exceptions reported to Dynatrace RUM, simulating a bad client-side deploy and spiking the RUM JavaScript error rate without breaking the page.

frontendLayoutShift.description: Frontend/RUM demo: the storefront injects a late-expanding banner that pushes content down after paint, degrading the Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) Core Web Vital captured by Dynatrace RUM.

accountingIdleInTransaction.description: Data-tier demo: the accounting Kafka consumer holds each write transaction open and idle for the given number of seconds after persisting an order, reproducing idle-in-transaction backends that hold row locks, block autovacuum on the accounting tables, and back up Kafka consumer lag. The variant is the hold time in seconds.
